[Introduction to Machine Learning on AWS Course Lesson | QA Platform](https://app.qa.com/course/introduction-to-machine-learning-on-aws-learning-path/intro-to-ml-on-aws-learning-path/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)

 machine learning as a field of study that gives computers the ability to learn without being explicitly programmed. 

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)>

A computer program is said to learn from experience E with respect to some class of tasks T and performance measure P, if its performance at tasks in T, as measured by P, improves with experience E. 

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)>

Features, each item or piece of data is described by a number of features.

Labels, again, each item or piece of data may be tagged with a known classification result.

And Instances, an instance or sample is an item within the dataset, each instance will have a set of features, and may also have a label assigned to it.

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/machine-learning-concepts/basic-concepts/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)>

Supervised learning

- Classification - specific categories
- Regression - continuous space

Unsupervised

- Clustering

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Unsupervised

- Clusters
- Anomaly detection
- Association discovery - feature rules

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

SVM finds lines that splits groups

K-Nearest Neighbours

Find similar or close items.

Tanh is zero centred so preferred over sigmoid

Relu is much simpler, so less complex math is required.

Real world applications

Object recognition can be better than humans.

Deep face, captioning images for blind people.

D RNN, speech recognition and synthesis

Language translation, google translate

Diagnosis or retinal disease.

Diagnosing skin cancer.

Controlling cooling of data centre. 40% reduction in cost.

Agriculture cucumber sorted, good from bad

Self driving cars

Alpha Go

Video games - reinforcement learning

Art - music and images.

Transfer style of one painting to anther

Anaconda 3 python

git clone [https://github.com/Dataweekends/zero_to_deep_learning_video.git](https://github.com/Dataweekends/zero_to_deep_learning_video.git)

Moral concerns

Profiling

Quality of data

Would you want to automate stuff like cancer diagnoses, you probably want a human to be involved.

Big data

Volume - TB

Velocity - 100 Mb/s

Variety

Categorical

- Nominal - symbol
- Ordinal - rankable

Notation

Features x, X

Target/label y

Y hat is prediction

Binary classification is often -1 and 1

EC2 environment

Nohup allows for notebook to run even if ssh is terminated. And output is to nohup.out

ssh -i key.pem ubuntu@localhost -fN -L 0.0.0.0:8000:127.0.0.1:8888

The -fN creates the tunnel in the background and the 0.0.0.0 tells it to listen on all interfaces instead of only 127.0.0.1/localhost so you can connect from the public IP

From <[https://app.qa.com/lab/analyzing-cpu-vs-gpu-performance-for-aws-machine-learning/session-page/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/lab/analyzing-cpu-vs-gpu-performance-for-aws-machine-learning/session-page/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)>

View nvidia

watch -n 1 nvidia-smi

First GPU run was very slow. Maybe there is work setting it up. So for small tasks GPU might actually be slower.

The first time you run the cell it takes significantly longer than following times because the code is being compiled and libraries are being loaded, and the code is loaded onto the GPU. The first output you see will be warning messages:

The CPU time grows proportional to the size of the matrix squared or cubed. That is, doubling the matrix size results in a 4X to 8X increase in compute time. This follows the growth in the number of computations required to calculate matrix products of different sizes

The GPU time grows almost linearly with the size of the matrix for the sizes used in the experiment. That is doubling the size doubles the time. This indicates that the GPU hasn't reached capacity. It is able to add more compute cores to complete the computation in much shorter times than a CPU.

The compute time for matrices of size less than 1000 is similar for GPU and CPU. Sometimes the CPU performs better than GPU for these small sizes. Transferring results from GPU to CPU and initializing GPU programs create some overhead in using a GPU. In general, GPUs excel for large-scale problems.

The speedup increases with the matrix size. The maximum reached is around 20X. A 20X speedup can mean a compute job finishing in 1 day on CPU, or in 1 hour on GPU. For larger problems, GPUs can offer speedups in the hundreds.

In this lab step, you analyzed the results of the experiment and gained an understanding of when GPUs can offer substantial improvements and when they are overkill. For a complete comparison, you need to consider the cost of running GPU instances to running CPU-only instances. Usually, if you have problems large enough to be compute-bound, the price increase of using GPUs is significantly less than the speedup achieved by using GPUs. This results in a reduced overall price.

For 10000x10000 started to max out, and temp slowly started to increase.

For very large problems, you can utilize multiple GPUs in a cluster. AWS provides [a machine learning CloudFormation template, opens in a new tab](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/distributed-deep-learning-made-easy/) to help with setting up an auto-scaled cluster of GPUs for machine learning tasks.

From <[https://app.qa.com/lab/analyzing-cpu-vs-gpu-performance-for-aws-machine-learning/session-page/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/lab/analyzing-cpu-vs-gpu-performance-for-aws-machine-learning/session-page/?context_id=126&context_resource=lp)>
